title: Rust Full Path
description: Large Rust change (> 100 lines, cross-file) or complex refactor. Plan gate, full PR checklist, modum, deep self-review. Stops at PR — user handles CI, review, merge.

tasks.title: Context Read
tasks.description: Read relevant Rust source code. Understand the problem, affected modules, function signatures, and callers. Check related tests. Research architecture context.

tasks.title: ⏸ GATE: Approve Plan
tasks.description: Human gate. Present the implementation plan to the user. Wait for explicit approval before proceeding.

tasks.title: Verify branch + rebase to origin/main
tasks.description:
Verify branch is new: `git branch -r | grep <branch-name>` — if it exists on any remote, rename (add `-v2`).

Fetch and rebase onto origin/main (NOT upstream/main — they have diverged; upstream/main has commits not in origin/main):
```
git fetch origin
git rebase origin/main
```
Verify: `git diff origin/main --stat` shows ONLY the files you intend to change.
acceptance_criteria: branch name is unique across remotes
git diff origin/main --stat shows only intended files

tasks.title: Write tests
tasks.description: Add test cases for the new behaviour. Rust tests live in the same file (unit tests, `#[cfg(test)] mod tests`) or `tests/` directory (integration tests). Every new code path should have a test. Edge cases (empty, null, None, overflow) must be covered.

tasks.title: Implement
tasks.description: Write the code change. Keep it scoped to one issue — one logical change per PR. Do NOT bundle unrelated fixes.

tasks.title: modum check
tasks.description: Run `modum check --mode error` on the crate root. Fix violations before proceeding.

tasks.title: Deep self-review
tasks.description:
Read the ENTIRE diff (`git diff origin/main`) line by line. Check:
- Logic errors: Does the control flow handle all edge cases? (early returns, None/Err paths, fallthroughs, type conversions)
- **Unchanged call sites: Did you update ALL callers of a refactored function? Grep for old signatures everywhere — this is the most common failure pattern**
- Message correctness: Do user-facing strings reference the right flags, paths, and commands?
- Edge cases: What happens when a dependency is missing, a subprocess fails, a config is empty?
- Consistency: Are variable names, function signatures, and arg ordering consistent across the change?
- Test coverage: Do the tests actually test the new behaviour, or just the old one with renamed variables?
acceptance_criteria: ALL callers of changed functions updated

tasks.title: Fix self-review findings
tasks.description: Address any issues found during self-review. Then re-run modum.

tasks.title: Verify PR diff is clean
tasks.description:
`git diff origin/main --stat`. Must show ONLY the intended changed files. If it shows hundreds of files, the branch is based on the wrong branch — abort and start fresh:
```
git checkout origin/main -b fix/your-fix-v2
```
Do not try to fix a dirty branch by rebasing. Start fresh from origin/main.

tasks.title: Push + PR
tasks.description:
Push to upstream (NOT origin): `git push upstream <branch>`

Create PR: `gh pr create --repo tmih06/stemcell --head leshchenko1979:<branch>`

Immediately verify PR diff: `gh pr diff --name-only` should match expected files. If it doesn't, close the PR and start over from origin/main.
